vocab.meaning: chi một khoản tiền lớn (thường là ngoài ý muốn)
vocab.example: I had to fork out a fortune for these designer trainers, but the durability is second to none.

vocab.meaning: chặt chém, bán giá quá đắt
vocab.example: Some high-end brands really rip you off, charging hundreds of dollars for shoes that cost very little to make.

vocab.meaning: mua nhanh chóng (vì giá hời hoặc hàng hiếm)
vocab.example: Whenever there's a seasonal sale at Nike, fans usually snap up the latest models within minutes.

vocab.meaning: khảo giá, so sánh nhiều cửa hàng trước khi mua
vocab.example: Before committing to a pair of luxury loafers, I like to shop around to find the best deal online.

vocab.meaning: mang giày mới cho mềm ra và vừa chân
vocab.example: Brand-name leather boots can be stiff at first, so it takes a few weeks to properly wear them in.

vocab.meaning: tiêu tốn của ai đó bao nhiêu tiền
vocab.example: These limited-edition sneakers set me back nearly five hundred dollars, but they are a great investment.
To be honest, many people buy branded footwear just to show off their social status.
I've decided to do away with fast-fashion shoes and focus on high-quality, sustainable brands instead.

vocab.meaning: hư hỏng, tan nát (sau thời gian sử dụng)
vocab.example: Cheap replicas often fall apart after a month, which is why I prefer to invest in authentic labels.

vocab.meaning: tiết kiệm tiền (để mua thứ gì đó cụ thể)
vocab.example: I'm currently saving up for a pair of high-performance running shoes from a professional athletic brand.
